_comment: Reviewed pre-existing raw-owner worker captures. Each needle must be part of the matched thread construction and carries the teardown invariant that makes the site acceptable. New sites fail raw_this_async_check.py until reviewed.

entries.reason: BufferingReader destructor calls stop and joins the worker before member teardown
SampleStreamDecodePool release stops and joins every worker before clearing worker, source, and configuration storage
AlsaDevice destructor stops and joins the IO thread before closing device state
WasapiDevice destructor calls stop before close; the self-thread branch retires the loop before detach
StreamingSampleSource release stops and joins the reader thread before clearing the reader
BonjourBackend destructor stops DNS services and joins both process-loop threads
WindowsBonjourBackend destructor stops and joins browse and registration threads
ConnectedChildProcess owns a dedicated join-or-reap teardown path for its monitor worker
ChildProcessManager start joins this scoped IPC-server worker on every return path before the captured stack flags leave scope
IPC read and accept loops have explicit stop or disconnect teardown; read-loop post-callback dereferences are AliveToken guarded
MountedVolumeListChangeDetector destructor stops and joins the polling thread
BackgroundTaskLane destructor stops and joins its single worker
ReloadableShell destructor stops and joins its watcher before Processor teardown
GpuAudioTransport shutdown joins its worker before transport storage is released

entries.needle: std::thread([this, worker = std::move(worker), progress = std::move(progress), completion = std::move(completion)]
entries.reason: BackgroundScanner destructor invokes stop_and_join and the callbacks are moved into the worker
AlsaMidiInput destructor closes the handle and joins the read thread
BluezBleMidiCentral stops and joins the worker before disconnecting owned devices
LinuxEnvObserver is process-static and its destructor signals and joins the poll thread
Windows environment observer is intentionally process-lived and its worker is documented as process-lived
WindowsRenderLoop and TimerRenderLoop destructors stop and join their worker threads
AsyncStream destructor calls stop; existing writer and reader workers remain tracked lifecycle debt
UdevMonitor destructor signals its wake pipe and joins before releasing udev and library state
WebSocketChannel factory transfers unique ownership only after starting the reader and destructor closes then joins it
